name: New Mexico SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits to eligible low-income New Mexico residents through an EBT card.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 165% FPL under broad-based categorical eligibility

name: New Mexico Medicaid (Centennial Care)
description: State Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage through managed care for low-income New Mexicans including children, families, elderly, and disabled individuals.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 235% FPL; pregnant women up to 250% FPL

name: New Mexico Department of Workforce Solutions - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to New Mexico workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: New Mexico Mortgage Finance Authority (MFA)
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homelessness prevention, down payment assistance, and Housing Choice Vouchers.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: New Mexico Legal Aid
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income New Mexicans in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and Native American law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income New Mexico residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: New Mexico Workforce Connection Centers
description: State workforce development centers providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, skills training, and employer connections statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Any New Mexico resident seeking employment or career development services

name: New Mexico LIHEAP - Home Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible New Mexico households pay heating and cooling bills through the Human Services Department.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: New Mexico 211
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting New Mexicans with local health and human services resources around the clock.
eligibilitySummary: Any New Mexico resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Roadrunner Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate and the largest food bank in New Mexico distributing food through hundreds of partner agencies including pantries, shelters, and meal programs statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across New Mexico

name: The Food Depot - Northern New Mexico Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving northern New Mexico distributing food to partner agencies across a large rural region.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in northern New Mexico

name: New Mexico Department of Veterans Services
description: Assists New Mexico veterans and their families with accessing state and federal benefits, claims assistance, healthcare referrals, and the state veterans home.
eligibilitySummary: New Mexico veterans and their families

name: New Mexico Crisis and Access Line
description: Statewide 24/7 behavioral health crisis line providing immediate support, crisis intervention, and referrals for mental health and substance abuse emergencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in New Mexico experiencing a mental health or substance abuse crisis

name: New Mexico Child Care Assistance Program
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working families, families in training, and TANF recipients afford quality childcare for their children.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under 13 needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 350% FPL (one of the most generous in the nation)

name: New Mexico WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: New Mexico TANF Cash Assistance
description: New Mexico Works program providing temporary cash assistance and employment support services to low-income families with children.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TANF payment standard for family size

name: ABQ RIDE - Albuquerque Public Transit
description: Public bus system serving the Albuquerque metropolitan area with fixed routes, Rapid Ride BRT, Sun Van paratransit, and reduced fares for eligible riders.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Albuquerque metro area; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and youth

name: New Mexico Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income New Mexico households to reduce energy costs and improve comfort.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: New Mexico Opportunity Scholarship
description: Tuition-free college program covering tuition and fees at any New Mexico public college or university for qualifying residents regardless of income.
eligibilitySummary: New Mexico residents enrolled at least half-time in a public college or university
incomeLimitNotes: No income limit; available to all qualifying residents

name: New Mexico Aging and Long-Term Services Department
description: Provides services for seniors including home-delivered meals, caregiver support, adult day care, and long-term care coordination to help elderly New Mexicans remain independent.
eligibilitySummary: New Mexico residents age 60 and older and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Some services have income requirements; others available to all seniors

name: New Mexico Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services for New Mexicans with disabilities including vocational counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: New Mexico residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: Albuquerque Community Safety - Family and Community Services
description: City of Albuquerque department providing social services including emergency housing, domestic violence services, senior services, and community center programs.
eligibilitySummary: Albuquerque residents in need of social services and community programs

name: DNA People's Legal Services
description: Provides free legal services to low-income residents of the Navajo Nation and other Native American communities in New Mexico, Arizona, and Utah.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of the Navajo Nation and Native American communities
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: New Mexico Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence throughout New Mexico.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence in New Mexico

name: New Mexico Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers
description: Federally funded rental assistance vouchers administered through local housing authorities and MFA to help very low-income families afford safe housing.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Generally household income at or below 50% of area median income

name: New Mexico Adult Education
description: Free adult education programs including GED preparation, English language classes, and basic literacy offered through schools and community organizations statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: New Mexico Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income New Mexicans to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: New Mexico Rail Runner Express
description: Commuter rail service connecting Belen, Albuquerque, and Santa Fe providing affordable regional transportation along the central Rio Grande corridor.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone traveling along the Belen-Albuquerque-Santa Fe corridor

name: Health Insurance Marketplace - beWellnm
description: New Mexico's state-based health insurance exchange where residents can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium subsidies.
eligibilitySummary: New Mexico residents without employer coverage or qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL
hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM MT; extended hours during open enrollment

name: Santa Fe Community Services - Housing Programs
description: City of Santa Fe housing assistance programs including emergency rental assistance, affordable housing, and homelessness prevention for Santa Fe residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income Santa Fe residents seeking housing assistance

name: New Mexico Indian Affairs Department
description: State agency coordinating services between tribal governments and state agencies, providing resources and advocacy for New Mexico's 23 tribes, pueblos, and nations.
eligibilitySummary: Members of New Mexico's tribal nations, pueblos, and Native American communities

name: Las Cruces Community of Hope - Homeless Services
description: Campus of service providers in Las Cruces offering shelter, meals, case management, healthcare, and job training for individuals experiencing homelessness.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in the Las Cruces area
